# px0 - Open Source Prompt Infrastructure

> Prompts are code. Treat them like it. Stop hardcoding prompts. px0 brings versioned template management, in-process caching, A/B testing, and OpenTelemetry prompt observability to your AI stack so you can iterate faster and deploy with confidence.

## Core Philosophy

Modern AI applications suffer from prompt spaghetti where prompt text is hardcoded across dozens of codebase files. Updating a single sentence requires a git PR, code review, CI/CD run, and a service restart.

px0 decouples prompts from application code:

- Prompt Registry: Prompts live as version-controlled templates.
- Standardized SDKs: Applications render templates using an ultra-simple SDK available in Python, Node.js, and Go.
- Instant Rollout: Push changes live without deploying code.
- OTEL Observability: Trace and evaluate every prompt render.

## Technical Features

### No Hardcoded Strings

- Decoupled Architecture: Prompts live in version-controlled templates, not scattered across your codebase.
- Searchability: No more nested search-and-replace to update prompt logic.

### Standardized SDK Integration

- Unified API signatures: Use the same patterns across Python, Node.js, and Go.
- Simple Rendering: A single render function abstracts all communication and cache-management logic.
- Flexible Configuration: Swap template names or roll out dynamic version flags without updating calling code.

### Deploy-Free Updates

- Zero Pipeline Overhead: Push a prompt template version update and it goes live instantly.
- No Rebuilds: No git commits, pull requests, CI/CD pipelines, or container restarts are needed to update a prompt.

### Advanced Sandboxed Templating

- Jinja Syntax: Prompts are formulated using standard Jinja2 syntax.
- Full Control: Supports advanced language constructs including variables, conditionals, loops, custom filters, and reusable macros.
- Safe Execution: Sandboxed rendering ensures untrusted inputs or bad structures never crash the main application.

### Atomic Versioning and Rollbacks

- Immutable Revisions: Every published prompt template version is immutable.
- Atomic Deployments: Changes are rolled out atomically across all active SDK instances.
- Instant Recovery: Roll back to any previous healthy template version in one click.

### High Performance In-Process Caching

- Low Latency: Less than 2 milliseconds latency overhead on critical paths.
- Local Storage: Prompts are cached in the application memory to avoid remote queries on critical paths.
- Asynchronous Refresh: Automatic background TTL refresh before the cache expires keeps prompts fresh without blocking execution.
- Warm Start: Cache pre-warming on startup avoids cold-start latency.

### Dynamic A/B Testing

- Traffic Splitting: Split prompt-rendering traffic dynamically across multiple template versions using percentage allocations.
- Data-Driven Decisions: Measure performance differences and let user engagement metrics determine the winning prompt.

### Native OpenTelemetry Observability

- Built-In Tracing: Every prompt rendering is traced and measured natively.
- Standard Exporters: Supports out-of-the-box exporting for latency, cache hit rate, and render error metrics.
- Multi-Backend Integration: Send tracing spans and metric data to any standard OpenTelemetry collector or backend.

### Draft and Audit Workflows

- Lifecycle Management: Draft, review, and publish cycles ensure that prompt revisions undergo checks.
- Comparison Tooling: Interactive visual diffs between any two template versions.
- Governance: Comprehensive audit trail of every change capturing what was changed, by whom, and when.

## SDK Integration Examples

Below are standard examples demonstrating how a Jinja template is set up and rendered across various backend SDKs.

### Prompt Template: customer_support.j2

```jinja
{# customer_support - v7 #}
{% set tone = "formal" if user.tier == "enterprise" else "friendly" %}

You are a {{ tone }} assistant for {{ company.name }}.
User: {{ query }}
Lang: {{ lang | default("English") }}
```

### Python SDK Usage

```python
import px0

# Render the prompt template directly
prompt = px0.render("customer_support", {
    "user": user,
    "company": company,
    "query": query,
})
```

### Node.js SDK Usage

```typescript
import { px0 } from "px0";

// Render prompt template asynchronously
const prompt = await px0.render("customer_support", {
  user,
  company,
  query,
});
```

### Go SDK Usage

```go
import "github.com/px0-ai/px0-go"

// Render the prompt template using maps
prompt, err := px0.Render("customer_support",
    map[string]any{
        "user":    user,
        "company": company,
        "query":   query,
    },
)
```

## Project Status

- Status: px0 is actively building its core platform. Early access and private hosting options are available.
- GitHub Repository: [github.com/px0-ai/px0](https://github.com/px0-ai/px0)
